_: A young man in his early twenties, guard's tabard still on, sitting at the bar with a half-finished ale. Sword belt unbuckled on the stool beside him. Eyes scanning the room — a habit, not a choice.

1._: Appearance. What anyone in the room can see.
1.1: Lean, angular face. Dark hair cropped short in the guard style. A thin scar across the left cheek — recent, still pink. Sits with one foot on the floor, ready to stand.
1.2: Off-duty guard tabard, worn but clean. Leather boots with harbour-road mud on the soles. The sword belt on the stool beside him is regulation issue — plain leather, functional scabbard.
1.3: Fidgets. Taps the bar with one finger. Glances at the front door every time the draught changes. Trained to watch but hasn't learned to do it subtly.

2._: Kael. Town guard, three months on rotation in Thornkeep. Posted here from the inland garrison at Greymarch. Youngest on the watch.
2.1: Trained fighter — southern school, speed over power. Quick draw, good footwork, poor at sustained grappling. Better in open space than tight quarters.
2.2: Reads. Literate in three scripts. Recognises codes, ciphers, guild marks. This is unusual for a guard and he doesn't advertise it. Noticed the bronze guild mark on recent toll receipts differs from last season's.
2.3: Poor with animals. Horses dislike him. Has been bitten twice at the garrison stables. Avoids the harbour cats.

3._: Purpose. What drives Kael beneath the surface.
3.1: Clear his family's name. His father was accused of forging trade documents. The accusation was false — Kael is certain — but the family lost standing. He requested the Thornkeep posting because the trade route passes through here.
3.2: Find the merchant who sold the forged documents that were attributed to his father. The guild mark on those documents matches the one now appearing on toll receipts at Greymarch crossing.
3.3: Prove himself to Captain Harren. Three months on watch and still given gate duty. Wants a patrol assignment. Wants to be taken seriously.
